pokemon:cloyster.name: Cloyster
pokemon:cloyster.summary: Cloyster is capable of swimming in the sea. It does so by swallowing water, then jetting it out toward the rear. This Pokémon shoots spikes from its shell using the same system.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Cloyster is capable of swimming in the sea. It does so by swallowing water, then jetting it out toward the rear. This Pokémon shoots spikes from its shell using the same system.
It fights by keeping its shell tightly shut for protection and by shooting spikes to repel foes.
It fights by keeping its shell tightly shut for protection and by shooting spikes to repel foes.
When attacked, it launches its horns in quick volleys. Its innards have never been seen.
Even a missile can't break the spikes it uses to stab opponents. They're even hard­ er than its shell.
It fights by keeping its shell tightly shut for protection and by shooting spikes to repel foes.
It swims in the sea by swallowing water, then jetting it out toward the rear. The CLOYSTER shoots spikes from its shell using the same system.
Its shell is extremely hard. It cannot be shattered, even with a bomb. The shell opens only when it is attacking.
Once it slams its shell shut, it is impossible to open, even by those with superi­ or strength.
Once it slams its shell shut, it is impossible to open, even by those with superior strength.
When attacked, it launches its horns in quick volleys. Its innards have never been seen.
For protection, it uses its harder-than-diamond shell. It also shoots spikes from the shell.
For protection, it uses its harder-than-diamond shell. It also shoots spikes from the shell.
Excavation of the tombs of ancient hunting tribes has turned up many spears tipped with spikes that had fallen off this Pokémon’s shell.
Cloyster is capable of swimming in the sea. It does so by swallowing water, then jetting it out toward the rear. This Pokémon shoots spikes from its shell using the same system.
It fights by keeping its shell tightly shut for protection and by shooting spikes to repel foes.
It fights by keeping its shell tightly shut for protection and by shooting spikes to repel foes.
When attacked, it launches its horns in quick volleys. Its innards have never been seen.
CLOYSTER is capable of swimming in the sea. It does so by swallowing water, then jetting it out toward the rear. This POKéMON shoots spikes from its shell using the same system.
CLOYSTER is capable of swimming in the sea. It does so by swallowing water, then jetting it out toward the rear. This POKéMON shoots spikes from its shell using the same system.
Once it slams its shell shut, it is impossible to open, even by those with superior strength.
CLOYSTER that live in seas with harsh tidal currents grow large, sharp spikes on their shells.
CLOYSTER that live in seas with harsh tidal currents grow large, sharp spikes on their shells.
Its hard shell cannot be shattered—not even by a bomb. The contents of the shell remain unknown.
Its shell is extremely hard. It cannot be shattered, even with a bomb. The shell opens only when it is attacking.
Slowpoke tails are its favorite food. It has even been known to come up on land to look for Slowpoke from time to time.
If areas of Cloyster’s very hard shell get damaged, those areas swell, gradually growing into large sharp spikes.
It fights by keeping its shell tightly shut for protection and by shooting spikes to repel foes.
It fights by keeping its shell tightly shut for protection and by shooting spikes to repel foes.
Cloyster that live in seas with harsh tidal currents grow large, sharp spikes on their shells.
Its shell is extremely hard. It cannot be shattered, even with a bomb. The shell opens only when it is attacking.
For protection, it uses its harder- than-diamonds shell. It also shoots spikes from the shell.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Cloyster is capable of swimming in the sea. It does so by swallowing water, then jetting it out toward the rear. This Pokémon shoots spikes from its shell using the same system.

pokemon:shellder.name: Shellder
pokemon:shellder.summary: At night, this Pokémon uses its broad tongue to burrow a hole in the seafloor sand and then sleep in it. While it is sleeping, Shellder closes its shell, but leaves its tongue hanging out.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: At night, this Pokémon uses its broad tongue to burrow a hole in the seafloor sand and then sleep in it. While it is sleeping, Shellder closes its shell, but leaves its tongue hanging out.
It swims backward by opening and closing its two shells. Its large tongue is always kept hanging out.
It swims backward by opening and closing its two shells. Its large tongue is always kept hanging out.
Its hard shell repels any kind of attack. It is vulnerable only when its shell is open.
Clamping on to an opponent reveals its vulnerable parts, so it uses this move only as a last resort.
It swims backward by opening and closing its two shells. Its large tongue is always kept hanging out.
At night, it burrows a hole in the seafloor with its broad tongue to make a place to sleep. While asleep, it closes its shell, but leaves its tongue hanging out.
It is encased in a shell that is harder than diamond. Inside, however, it is surprisingly tender.
It swims facing backward by open­ ing and closing its two-piece shell. It is surprisingly fast.
It swims facing backward by opening and closing its two-piece shell. It is surprisingly fast.
Its hard shell repels any kind of attack. It is vulnerable only when its shell is open.
The shell can withstand any attack. However, when it is open, the tender body is exposed.
The shell can withstand any attack. However, when it is open, the tender body is exposed.
This Pokémon’s tongue is always hanging out. It uses its tongue with great dexterity to dig up sand from the seabed in its search for food.
At night, this Pokémon uses its broad tongue to burrow a hole in the seafloor sand and then sleep in it. While it is sleeping, Shellder closes its shell, but leaves its tongue hanging out.
It swims backward by opening and closing its two shells. Its large tongue is always kept hanging out.
It swims backward by opening and closing its two shells. Its large tongue is always kept hanging out.
Its hard shell repels any kind of attack. It is vulnerable only when its shell is open.
At night, this POKéMON uses its broad tongue to burrow a hole in the seafloor sand and then sleep in it. While it is sleeping, SHELLDER closes its shell, but leaves its tongue hanging out.
At night, this POKéMON uses its broad tongue to burrow a hole in the seafloor sand and then sleep in it. While it is sleeping, SHELLDER closes its shell, but leaves its tongue hanging out.
Its hard shell repels any kind of attack. It is vulnerable only when its shell is open.
Grains of sand trapped in its shells mix with its body fluids to form beautiful pearls.
Grains of sand trapped in its shells mix with its body fluids to form beautiful pearls.
The hardness of its shell surpasses the hardness of a diamond. In days gone by, people used the shells to make shields.
It swims facing backward by opening and closing its two-piece shell. It is surprisingly fast.
Even when its shell is closed, its tongue still hangs out. If you give its tongue a good yank, the shock will cause Shellder to open its shell.
The sand that accumulates inside its shell eventually becomes a pearl. But the pearl gets in the way, so it spits it out and discards it.
It swims backward by opening and closing its two shells. Its large tongue is always kept hanging out.
It swims backward by opening and closing its two shells. Its large tongue is always kept hanging out.
Its hard shell repels any kind of attack. It is vulnerable only when its shell is open.
Clamping on to an opponent reveals its vulnerable parts, so it uses this move only as a last resort.
The shell can withstand any attack. However, when it is open, the tender body is exposed.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: At night, this Pokémon uses its broad tongue to burrow a hole in the seafloor sand and then sleep in it. While it is sleeping, Shellder closes its shell, but leaves its tongue hanging out.
